message:
fix(sync): prevent baton loss in Notified drop when notify_one races with broadcast

When a Notified future was dropped after being woken by notify_one but
before it could poll, and a broadcast (notify_waiters) had also bumped
the generation, the baton was silently lost. The dropping future would
see the generation change, remove itself from the waiters list, and
return — but no other waiter would be woken to receive the notify_one
signal.

Fix the drop path to check whether the waiter was individually notified
(entry.notified flag) at its original generation before a broadcast
changed the generation. If so, the drop path now calls pass_baton()
to wake the next waiter, preserving the notify_one guarantee.

Consolidate the two-phase generation check (unlocked then locked) into
a single locked check that avoids redundant lock acquisition. The
is_gen_changed flag is computed once under the lock.

Update notify_bug_test.rs with additional assertions verifying:
- The dropped waiter's baton is passed to the next waiter
- The test explicitly sequences notify_one → broadcast → waiter drop
  to reproduce the race condition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
